content.full_text:
August 5, 2004

The Honorable Simón Salinas
California Assembly
Post Office Box 942849
Sacramento, CA 94249-0028

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-147

Dear Assemblymember Salinas:

This letter is in response to your request regarding the Agricultural Worker Health and Housing Commission. Your request for advice concerns commissioners appointed to the Agricultural Worker Health and Housing Commission pursuant to ACR 236 (Chapter 178 – 2002) and whether they are required to file statements of economic interests under the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).1

The Fair Political Practices Commission can only provide written advice to a person regarding his or her own duties under the Act, or to that person’s authorized representative. (Section 83114; regulation 18329, copy enclosed.) Therefore, we must withdraw your request for advice. However, as part of the FPPC’s duties as the code reviewing body for state agency conflict-of-interest codes, the staff monitors the creation of new agencies such as this new commission for the purpose of determining whether they are required to adopt a conflict-of-interest code.

1 Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

Thank you for your inquiry. If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
Chief, Technical Assistance Division
